summary: Joel Spolsky highlights Maxim Shemanarev's work on font rasterization, sharing a quote that emphasizes how great engineering results come from applying publicly available knowledge with engineering intuition and common sense rather than breakthrough innovations. The brief post serves as a pointer to Shemanarev's detailed technical article, with Joel's single-word reaction ('Wow!') conveying deep admiration for the craftsmanship on display.
key_points: Great engineering often requires no novel breakthroughs — just careful application of existing knowledge
Engineering intuition and common sense can produce remarkable results without patents or rocket science
Craftsmanship in software is about patience and attention to detail, not flashy innovation
money_quotes: The amazing thing is there is no rocket science! Nothing to patent!
You only need to use a bit of your engineering intuition plus common sense.
key_insight: Outstanding software craftsmanship comes not from proprietary breakthroughs but from patiently applying engineering intuition and common sense to publicly available knowledge.
